meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with this expert-led guide. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first portfolio-ready AI application today.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. Use Antigravity to architect your project scope and Claude Code to execute the boilerplate instantly. By pairing AI-assisted planning with rapid CLI-based development, you bridge the gap between 'no ideas' and a functional, deployable application. This workflow turns abstract curiosity into concrete code in minutes.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the student developer's greatest adversary. You have the curiosity, the drive, and the basic syntax knowledge, yet you find yourself staring at an empty folder, paralyzed by the overwhelming noise of 'what to build.' This isn't a failure of skill; it is a failure of workflow. 

We are shifting the paradigm. By leveraging Antigravity for structural ideation and Claude Code for terminal-based execution, you transform the development process from a grueling grind into a high-velocity sprint. It is time to stop thinking about your first project and start shipping it.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, use Antigravity to distill a complex idea into a manageable Minimum Viable Product that fits your skill level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Help me design a beginner-friendly SaaS tool that solves a specific problem for college students. Outline the core features, data schema, and a 3-day development roadmap.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Generate the folder structure and configuration files so you don't spend hours on setup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project file structure for a React + Node.js application. Include instructions for a clean folder hierarchy and essential environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the directory structure based on the architectural plan'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your primary functions based on the logic defined in your Antigravity session.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the backend logic for a user authentication system using JWT. Provide the code in clean, modular Node.js functions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the auth controller using the logic provided by the architect'

steps.title: Debug and Refactor
steps.description: Instead of hunting for bugs manually, feed the error messages directly into Claude Code for immediate remediation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this error stack trace and suggest a refactor that improves performance and readability while maintaining original functionality.

steps.title: Final Documentation
steps.description: Generate a professional README to ensure your project is portfolio-ready and easy for recruiters to understand.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a compelling project README that highlights the problem, the tech stack, and the specific challenges I overcame during development.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a professional README.md file in the root directory'

pro_tips: Always ask for 'modular, testable code' to ensure your project grows cleanly as you add new features.
Use Claude Code's terminal mode to automate repetitive shell commands like environment setup and dependency installation.
Treat your Antigravity session as a technical interview practice; frame your prompts to explain the 'why' behind your architectural choices.
Never accept the first code output; ask the AI to 'critique its own implementation' to find edge cases you might have missed.
Keep your context window focused by creating a new Claude session for every distinct feature module.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, deploy it to Vercel or Netlify and write a short blog post about the technical hurdles you overcame to add to your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap to bypass the beginner's block and start shipping professional-grade software. Stop learning in isolation and start building with a community that pushes you to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
